title: UTILITY ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric and gas bills in addition to heating fuel (oil, wood, propane). Appointment availability is limited. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Household income 60% Median Family Income or below Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. New appointments are made available at varying times and are added at least weekly. Appointments are scheduled at least one week in advance. Email (503) 946-9898 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 946-9898 Energy Assistance Line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call the energy assistance line to check for available appointments. When appointments are available, request an appointment through the automated phone system. If no appointments are available, callers are advised to check again the next day. Agency does not accept walk-ins. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -ID cards for household members age 18 and older -Social security cards -Income within the last 30 days (if paid on the day of intake bring check; no bank statements accepted) -If receiving SSD or SSI, bring Social Security Administration award letter -Most recent utility bill in the name of a household member LANGUAGES: English, Russian, Ukrianian AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 75 Services: Electric Service Payment Assistance Gas Service Payment Assistance Heating Fuel Payment Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - MICRO ENTERPRISE EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EARLY HEAD START EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - MANY NATIONS ACADEMY EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - COLLEGE AND CAREER SERVICES EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - HEALTH EQUITY HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - NAYA GENERATIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FOSTER CARE SUPPORT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - POSITIVE INDIAN PARENTING LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - CAMPS AND SEASONAL PROGRAMMING LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - CULTURAL ARTS LEISURE ACTIVITIES/RECREATION - RECREATION MUTUAL SUPPORT - HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CHXI SAN PLAYGROUP If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER
